NDRIs: different from SSRIs & SNRIs 💊🧠

NDRI = Norepinephrine + Dopamine Reuptake Inhibitor
(Think bupropion.)

Remember:
✔️ Used for depression & smoking cessation
✔️ NO sexual dysfunction (common test point 👀)
✔️ Can increase risk of seizures
✔️ Avoid in patients with eating

🩸 DKA Pathophysiology NCLEX Review
Diabetic ketoacidosis (DKA) is a life-threatening complication most commonly seen in type 1 diabetes mellitus. It occurs when there is little to no insulin available, causing glucose to remain in the bloodstream instead of entering cells.

recent_posts.caption:
SAS Method 🧼⁠
(Scrub, Administer, Scrub) ⁠
⁠
🧼Scrub/swab the IV or luer-lock with alcohol swabs for at least 15 seconds⁠
💉Administer IV push medication at the appropriate rate⁠
🧼Scrub again⁠
💧Flush with 0.9% sodium chloride (NaCl) at the same rate as the medication was pushe

Must-know Antidotes 💊⁠
⁠
What is an antidote? → An antidote is a medication or therapy that neutralizes or counteracts the effects of a drug or toxin in the body⁠
⁠
Does the NCLEX test on these? → YES! You’ll definitely see questions about antidotes in practice tests and on exam

What actually happens when a bag valve mask (BVM) is used on a patient? Overinflation forces air into the stomach and can cause serious complications. Slow squeeze, watch the chest rise, and you've got it. Save this for later! 🫁✅⁠
⁠
#nursingschool #clinicalskills #futurenurse #c

ECG/EKG interpretation study guide & workbook by Nurse Sarah. One of the biggest mistakes nursing students make when learning ECG interpretation is that they try to memorize what a rhythm looks like instead of understanding what makes each rhythm unique.

In this study guide, you

Nurse Fluid and Electrolytes Quiz:

What hypocalcemia sign is this?

Answer is D,Trousseau’s sign. This is associated with hypocalcemia (low blood calcium). Inflating a BP cuff above systolic pressure for several minutes increases neuromuscular excitability, producing that hand p

Blood Cells You Must Know for NCLEX & nursing:

WBCs (5 Types) = Fight Infection

-Monocytes = Street Cleaners (clean up during illness)
-Neutrophils = Rapid Response Team (1st to bacteria)
-Eosinophils = Parasite Hunters & Allergy Role
-Basophils = Allergy & Inflammation Team
-L

Routes to Take a Temperature (Most Common):

-Oral (mouth) = most common route; place under tongue (posterior sublingual pocket)

-Tympanic (ear) = place in ear per device instructions using provided cap/sheath

-Axillary (in armpit) = often 0.5 to 1°F LOWER than oral route

-Tem

Nurse pharmacology quiz for nursing school students and NCLEX:

A patient states they are experiencing an annoying, persistent dry cough. The patient is not experiencing any other signs or symptoms. As the nurse, you know that which medication below can cause this?

A. Atorvastat

Nursing cheat sheet: Before You Give These Meds, Check these Labs!

-Furosemide = Potassium (↓ K+)
-Spironolactone = Potassium (↑ K+)
-Digoxin = Digoxin level & Potassium (↓ K+ = toxicity)
-ACE Inhibitors/ARBs = Creatinine & K+ (↑ K+)
-Heparin = aPTT & Platelets
-Warfarin = PT/IN

How my "nurse brain" sees random things...

What do see that reminds you of something nursing-related?? LOL

#nursing #nurselife #funny #nurse #nursingstudent #nursingschool

Blood Pressure Reading Practice Challenge: What is this patient's blood pressure?

Comment your answer. Remember, the systolic pressure is the first tapping sound you hear, and the diastolic pressure is the point when the sound ceases.

This patient's blood pressure was 146/90 mm

Electrolyte ECG Changes:
HYPOkalemia (Potassium: < 3.5 mEq/L)
• Flat T waves
• U waves present
HYPERkalemia (Potassium: > 5.0 mEq/L)
• Tall T waves
HYPOcalcemia (Calcium: < 8.5 mg/dL)
• Long QT interval
HYPERcalcemia (Calcium: > 10.5 mg/dL)
• Short QT interval

🧠Memory Trick🧠

Floor Nursing Slang for New Nurses:
-Pushing meds = giving meds rapidly IV
-Vein rolled = vein shifted during needle stick 
-IV blew = vein failed; need to stick again 
-Hard stick = no good veins for IV/blood draw 
-Hemolyzed = blood sample unusable, need to redraw 
-Prime the l
